summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a fast‑paced ’81 show with standout Eyes of the World and high energy from the 80s era
review: The recording is a solid soundboard source, though some reviewers note vocals a bit forward and Jerry’s guitar disappearing until Tennessee Jed. Musically the show shines with blistering Eyes of the World, a lively Sugaree, strong Fire on the ... and Space, making it a must‑listen for fans of the early‑80s Dead. Overall the performance is energetic and enjoyable despite occasional tempo excess.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ~3.9, but most comments highlight strong performances and only minor mix quirks; weighting favors the show quality, yielding a solid 4‑star recommendation.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear soundboard capture with nice bass presence; some listeners find vocals a little front‑heavy and Jerry’s guitar low or absent in the early mix, but no major dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fast, high‑energy 1981 vibe; the set moves quickly with a particularly scorching Eyes of the World and tight transitions, though a few think the tempo runs a touch too fast.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar often low or missing in the mix early on, but pushes the pace on Eyes of the World when audible.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass praised as unusually solid for fall‑81 Europe; Space highlighted as a strong Phil moment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals/lyrics described as “blowing right and left,” indicating a weaker lyrical delivery.
